zh.Trenowyth Manor, St. Mewan, Cornwall, En: 英國可瓦爾聖 梅萬
zh.Bridgwater, Somerset, England: 英國薩默塞特布利其沃特
zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Hill於1400年9月26日出生在英國戴文金斯布利其時，她的父親Sir Robert Hill of Shilston Sr34歲，母親Isabella Wadham33歲。她於1424年與Thomas Carminow of Boconnoc在英國可瓦爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1427年在英國可瓦爾享年27歲。

zh-hans.Bridgwater, Somerset, England: 英国薩默塞特布利其沃特
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jane Hill于1400年9月26日出生在英国戴文金斯布利其时，她的父亲Sir Robert Hill of Shilston Sr34岁，母亲Isabella Wadham33岁。她于1424年与Thomas Carminow of Boconnoc在英国可瓦爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1427年在英国可瓦爾享年27岁。

nameMeanings.name: Hill
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived on or by a hill, from Middle English hill, hell, hull ‘hill’ (Old English hyll). Compare Hiller . This surname is also very common among African Americans.
English: possibly in some cases from the personal name Hille, a pet form of some name such as Hilger or Hillary .
German: from a short form of Hildebrand or any of a variety of other names, male and female, containing ancient Germanic hild ‘battle’ as the first element.

variants.name: Hillock
meanings: English and Scottish: topographic name for someone who lived ‘(by the) small hill’, from Middle English hillok, a diminutive of hille (Old English hyll ‘hill’). See Hill .

variants.name: Hills
meanings: English: variant of Hill with genitival -s if a relationship name, attested in Matilda Hilles, 1327 (Somerset), where the genitival form may signify widowhood; or with plural -s if habitational, attested in Johanne de Hyles, 1379 (Bickerstaffe, Lancashire), and Ricardo del Hyles, 1379 (Great Eccleston, Lancashire); or, with post-medieval excrescent -s, probably the most common form.

variants.name: Hiller
meanings: German: metronymic from Hille, a pet form of the female personal name Hildegund (see Hilke ).
German: from a masculine personal beginning with the ancient Germanic element hild ‘strife, battle’.

variants.name: Hil
meanings: Dutch (Van den Hil): topographic name from hil, hul ‘hill’, particularly in the sense of a flight hill above sea level in the southwestern Netherlands.

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Hill was born on 26 September 1400, in Kingsbridge, Devon, England, her father, Sir Robert Hill of Shilston Sr, was 34 and her mother, Isabella Wadham, was 33. She married Thomas Carminow of Boconnoc in 1424, in Cornwall, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She died in 1427, in Cornwall, England, at the age of 27.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane Hill est née le 26 septembre 1400, à Kingsbridge, Devon, Angleterre, son père, Sir Robert Hill of Shilston Sr, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Isabella Wadham, avait 33 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas Carminow of Boconnoc en 1424, à Cornouailles, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1427, à Cornouailles, Angleterre, à l’âge de 27 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jane Hill am 26. September 1400 in Kingsbridge, Devon, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Robert Hill of Shilston Sr 34 und ihre Mutter Isabella Wadham 33. Sie hat 1424 in Cornwall, England Thomas Carminow of Boconnoc geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1427 im Alter von 27 Jahren in Cornwall, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Hill è nata il il 26 settembre 1400 nel Kingsbridge, Devon, England, sua padre, Sir Robert Hill of Shilston Sr, aveva 34 anni e sua madre, Isabella Wadham, aveva 33 anni. Lei sposato Thomas Carminow of Boconnoc nel 1424, nel Cornwall, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1427, nel Cornwall, England, all'età di 27 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Jane Hillが1400年9月26日にイギリスデボンキングスブリッジで生まれた時，父Sir Robert Hill of Shilston Srは34歳，母Isabella Wadhamは33歳であった。1424年にイギリスコルンワルでThomas Carminow of Boconnocと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1427年にイギリスコルンワルで27歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Hill(이)가 1400년 9월 26일 영국 데폰 킨그스브리지에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Robert Hill of Shilston Sr(은)는 34세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Isabella Wadham(은)는 33세였다. 그녀는 1424년 영국 코른월에서 Thomas Carminow of Boconnoc(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1427년 영국 코른월에서 27세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Hill nasceu em 26 de setembro de 1400, em Kingsbridge, Devon, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Robert Hill of Shilston Sr, tinha 34 anos e sua mãe, Isabella Wadham, tinha 33 anos. Ela casou-se com Thomas Carminow of Boconnoc em 1424, em Cornwall, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1427, em Cornwall, Inglaterra, com 27 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane Hill родилась 26 сентября 1400 г. в местности кингсбридж, девон, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Robert Hill of Shilston Sr было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Isabella Wadham, 33. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Carminow of Boconnoc в 1424 г. в местности корнвалл, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1427 г. в местности корнвалл, Англия в возрасте 27 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jane Hill nació el 26 de septiembre de 1400, en Kingsbridge, Devon, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Robert Hill of Shilston Sr, tenía 34 años y su mamá, Isabella Wadham, tenía 33 años. Se casó con Thomas Carminow of Boconnoc en 1424, en Cornwall, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió en 1427, en Cornwall, Inglaterra, a la edad de 27 años.
